list.title: Pathways
list.description: Design care stages in the visual editor, publish when ready, and use each flow when you enroll and follow up patients.
list.newPathwayDialogDescription: Choose a name for this flow. Next, you can edit stages, alerts, and checklists on the following screen.
list.redirectWaitMessage: Pathway created. Opening the editor in a moment…
list.name: Name

detail.title: Pathway editor
detail.description: React Flow canvas — nodes become stages when published.

editor.pathwayNameRequired: Enter a pathway name before saving.
editor.draftHint: Use Save draft to persist the flow and name on the server. Publishing is only allowed when the saved draft differs from the already published version (and there is no unsaved local edits). Publishing makes this version active for patients and syncs stages.
editor.publishDisabledUnsavedTooltip: Save the draft first to enable publishing.
editor.publishDisabledNoChangesTooltip: The saved draft matches the published graph. Edit the flow, save, then publish — this avoids republishing with no real change.
editor.publishDisabledPreviewTooltip: See the red notice above: publishing is blocked.
editor.removeStageWithPatientsDescription: Stage «{name}» has {patientCount, plural, one {# patient} other {# patients}} on the published version. While they remain on it, publishing a flow without this stage will be blocked. You can remove it only in the draft and move patients before publishing.
editor.stagePropsDescription: Edit the name, time-based alerts, patient message, checklist, and documents sent to the patient when they enter the stage (e.g. WhatsApp). Stays in the draft until you publish.
editor.selectNode: Click a node on the canvas.
editor.removeStageAria: Remove selected stage from the flow
editor.cannotRemoveLastStage: At least one stage is required in the flow.
editor.defaultAssigneesDescription: Team members who are default owners for this stage (you can add several). On the patient journey, the first ID in the list that is still a tenant member is typically suggested as the stage owner; order is priority.
editor.defaultAssigneesHintShort: Optional. List order sets priority for the suggestion when entering the stage.
editor.defaultAssigneesEmpty: No default owners — the system falls back to the patient record assignee, if any.
editor.defaultAssigneesSearchNoResults: No team member matches your search.
editor.defaultAssigneesAllAdded: Every available member has already been added.
editor.patientMessageHint: Used by integrations when the patient enters this stage (e.g. WhatsApp).
editor.stageDocumentsTitle: Documents for the patient in this stage
editor.stageDocumentsDescription: PDFs and images from the clinic library are added to the bundle when the patient reaches this stage. Automatic delivery depends on your active integration (e.g. WhatsApp). Publish the pathway version for changes to take effect for patients.
editor.stageDocumentsUploadSuccess: {count, plural, =1 {1 file added to the stage.} other {{count} files added to the stage.}}

columnEditor.title: Treatment stages
columnEditor.description: Reorder columns with drag-and-drop. Order matches the dashboard Kanban after publish. Same data as the graph editor.
columnEditor.noPathways: No pathways yet. Create one under Journeys first.
columnEditor.listHint: Drag the handle to reorder. Save the draft, then publish to sync stages for patients.
columnEditor.removeStageWithPatientsDescription: Stage «{name}» has {patientCount, plural, one {# patient} other {# patients}} on the published version. While they remain on it, publishing without this stage will be blocked.
columnEditor.checklistDescription: Operational items used in the patient detail for this stage.
columnEditor.checklistRequiredForTransitionHint: Items with “Required to advance” enabled must be marked complete before moving the patient to the next stage (unless an audited override is used when allowed).
columnEditor.cannotRemoveLast: At least one stage is required.

patient.title: Patient journey
patient.description: Current stage and transition to the next published stage.
patient.sameStage: Pick a stage different from the current one.
